records.alert: Model is projecting 26,688 units over the next 26 weeks; the plan has 51,792 (25,104 over, -48%). If orders cool off as the model expects, that's overstock building at Amazon. This account doesn't order every week — they buy in bursts (12 weeks out of the last 13, roughly 1,183 units per order). Heads-up: the plan is a flat 1,992/wk placeholder — looks like a copy-paste rather than a week-by-week build off how Amazon actually orders.
records.ai_analysis:
Amazon POS Sales: LW 1,482u, L4W 1182/wk, L13W 1019/wk, L26W 833/wk, L52W 605/wk (accel).

Sales trend: ▲ up 16% L4W vs L13W; YoY +38%. Consumer demand is moving consistently across windows (L13W +22% vs L26W, YoY +38%) - this is real growth at Amazon, not a 1-off blip. Expect ordering pace to track POS unless something changes upstream.

Amazon DC inventory: SOH 4,400u · Open PO 6,396u · WOS 3.7wks.

AI 1,026/wk (26,688 total 26W) | Plan 1,992/wk (51,792 total) - plan is 48% above AI.
